# unifi_hotspot_voucher (Resource)

A batch of UniFi hotspot guest vouchers. Every voucher in the batch shares the same note and limits; the controller assigns each its own code. Vouchers cannot be updated in place, so any change forces a new batch.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# A batch of 10 guest WiFi vouchers, each valid for 24 hours (1440 minutes).
resource "unifi_hotspot_voucher" "guest_day_pass" {
  name               = "Guest day pass"
  quantity           = 10
  time_limit_minutes = 1440

  # Optional per-voucher limits; omit any for unlimited.
  data_usage_limit_mbytes = 5120
  rx_rate_limit_kbps      = 50000
  tx_rate_limit_kbps      = 10000
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) Voucher note, duplicated across all generated vouchers.
- `time_limit_minutes` (Number) How long (in minutes) the voucher grants access, counted from the first guest's authorization (1-1000000).

### Optional

- `authorized_guest_limit` (Number) Limit on how many different guests can use each voucher. Omit for unlimited.
- `data_usage_limit_mbytes` (Number) Per-voucher data usage limit in megabytes (1-1048576). Omit for unlimited.
- `quantity` (Number) Number of vouchers to generate (1-1000).
- `rx_rate_limit_kbps` (Number) Download rate limit in kilobits per second (2-100000). Omit for unlimited.
- `tx_rate_limit_kbps` (Number) Upload rate limit in kilobits per second (2-100000). Omit for unlimited.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) UUID of the first voucher in the batch; used as the resource identity.
- `vouchers` (List of Object) The generated vouchers, including their printable codes. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--vouchers))

<a id="nestedatt--vouchers"></a>
### Nested Schema for `vouchers`

Read-Only:

- `activated_at` (String)
- `authorized_guest_count` (Number)
- `code` (String)
- `created_at` (String)
- `expired` (Boolean)
- `expires_at` (String)
- `id` (String)
- `name` (String)
